title: The United Arab Emirates is caught in the fog of war
subhed: Surprised by the scale of Iran's attacks on their territory, Emirati leaders have worked to downplay the impact in order to retain the confidence of investors and tourists, while continuing to place orders for weapons.

title: Oil passes $100 a barrel following Strait of Hormuz blockage and Middle East war
subhed: The blockage of the Strait of Hormuz is forcing Persian Gulf countries to reduce their crude oil production. G7 leaders are considering jointly releasing their strategic reserves to supply the markets.

title: Israel's reservists, pillars of a nation growing accustomed to a protracted war
subhed: As part of the offensive against Iran, Israel has called up 100,000 soldiers. The impact is felt across society, but the country has grown used to these successive waves of mobilization.

title: 'War is far too sensitive an issue to be managed by artificial intelligence'
subhed: The standoff that erupted at the end of February in the US between Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and Dario Amodei, the head of Anthropic, highlights the urgent need to establish a political and legal framework capable of governing the use of technology, writes Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

title: Paris mayoral elections: What do the candidates propose?
subhed: Le Monde breaks down the platforms of the capital's six main candidates on safety, cleanliness, housing and the environment ahead of the French municipal elections on March 15 and 22.

Iranian school hit by air strike: Le Monde's investigation confirms many civilian victims, including children
subhed: Videos obtained and analyzed by Le Monde confirmed that the bombing of a school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of joint US-Israeli strikes on the country, caused many civilian victims, including children.

title: Thomas Piketty: 'In the face of Trump's militaristic drift and looming disaster, Europe must equip itself to have global influence'
subhed: Following the intervention in Venezuela, the war against Iran launched by the US president signals yet another admission of weakness by the United States, observes the economist in his column. This military agitation primarily serves to bolster the US economy.

title: What the Greek myth of Pandora reveals about the deep-seated fear of women
subhed: By opening a jar entrusted to her by the gods, Pandora becomes the instrument of their vengeance against rebellious humans and unleashes a torrent of plagues upon her fellow mortals. This misogynistic myth seems to contain all the resentments one half of humanity harbors against the other.
